title: dysoxylum blumei
intro: Dysoxylum blumei, also known as the Indonesian or Asian Dysoxylum, is a type of tree species belonging to the family Meliaceae, native to Southeast Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Dysoxylum blumei include its ability to treat various ailments such as fever, cough, and respiratory problems, as well as its potential to lower cholesterol and blood pressure levels. The plant is typically prepared as an herbal remedy, where the bark or leaves are dried and made into teas, infusions, or decoctions, and in some cases, it is also used in traditional folk medicine as a form of topical application or poultice.
